name: Knick v. Township of Scott
summary: Held that property owners may bring Fifth Amendment takings claims directly in federal court without first seeking compensation through state procedures, overruling Williamson County.
significance: GEO cited a footnote stating Yearsley was 'correct to recognize an immunity from suit.' Respondents countered that the next sentence says the Tucker Act provides a complete remedy excluding liability, showing 'immunity' was used loosely.
